errors.lineInputsNumbers: The x1, y1, x2, and y2 inputs must be numbers
errors.circleInputsNumbers: The x, y, and radius inputs must be numbers
errors.ellipseInputsNumbers: The x, y, rx, and ry inputs must be numbers
errors.triangleInputsNumbers: The x1, y1, x2, y2, x3, and y3 inputs must be numbers
errors.colorNotAllowed: Color should not be specified for this exercise
errors.colorNamedOrHex: Color must be a named color ("orange", "blue", "white", etc) specified in the instructions, or a hex color starting with #

buildSnowman.name: Build the snowman
buildSnowman.description: Draw three circles to build the snowman.

buildSnowman.name: Build the snowman
buildSnowman.description: Draw three circles to build the snowman.

howToDrawCircle.question: How do I draw a circle on the canvas?
howToDrawCircle.answer: Call `circle(centerX, centerY, radius)` where `centerX` and `centerY` are the center coordinates, and `radius` is how big it is.

circlesHorizontalPosition.question: Where should the circles be positioned horizontally?
circlesHorizontalPosition.answer: All three circles share the same horizontal center, so `centerX` is `50` for each (the canvas is `100` wide).

howToStackCircles.question: How do I stack the three circles?
howToStackCircles.answer: The base circle is the largest and sits near the bottom (high `centerY`). Each circle above has a smaller `centerY` and a smaller `radius`.

whatNumbersForRadii.question: What numbers should I use for the radii?
whatNumbersForRadii.answer: All numbers in this exercise are divisible by `5`. Try a base radius around `20`, a body around `15`, and a head around `10`, adjusting until they line up with the target.

circle.description: Draw a circle centered at (centerX, centerY) with the given radius
